Let’s begin by analyzing the problem. *highlighted section* We are given a rectangle with side lengths in the ratio $2:5$. If all sides are extended by $9 \text{ cm}$, the new ratio becomes $3:7$. *highlighted section* Our goal is to determine the new ratio of the side lengths after extending each side by an additional $9 \text{ cm}$. *highlighted section*

Let the original side lengths be $2x$ and $5x$, where $x$ is a positive real number. *highlighted section* When each side is extended by $9 \text{ cm}$, the new side lengths become $2x + 9$ and $5x + 9$. *highlighted section* According to the problem, the ratio of the new side lengths is $3:7$. 

We can set up the equation:

$$
\frac{2x + 9}{5x + 9} = \frac{3}{7}
$$

Cross-multiplying:

$$
7(2x + 9) = 3(5x + 9)
$$

$$
14x + 63 = 15x + 27
$$

$$
63 - 27 = 15x - 14x
$$

$$
36 = x
$$

*highlighted section* So, the original side lengths are $2x = 60 \text{ cm}$ and $5x = 150 \text{ cm}$. *highlighted section* After the first extension of $9 \text{ cm}$, the new side lengths are $69 \text{ cm}$ and $159 \text{ cm}$, which simplifies to the ratio $3:7$, as expected. *highlighted section*

Now, we are to extend each side by an additional $9 \text{ cm}$, making the new side lengths:

$$
69 + 9 = 78 \text{ cm} \quad \text{and} \quad 159 + 9 = 168 \text{ cm}
$$
